beats.narration: One casting, two thicknesses, one density window. Thin goes black, thick stays light, until you load two films in one cassette.

payload.title: TWO FILMS ONE SHOT
payload.sub: Two films in one cassette cover the whole part.

beats.narration: Two properties drive it. Speed: coarse grain is fast, fine grain slower but sharper. Latitude: flat curves cover more thickness, steep curves discriminate.
Load a fast and a slow film in one cassette with lead screens. Shoot once. Fast reads the heavy section; slow reads the thin.
Or load a same-speed pair. Stacked on the illuminator, densities add: two plus two reads four. Each film at least one point three alone.
The rule: every section lands in the window on at least one film; that's the only film you call it on.
